text: hi Sean here for BBC learning English
and today we're going to look at these
two little words so bean and gone are
both past participles both used with the
present perfect but they don't have the
same meaning we use bean to talk about
complete visits so she has been to Paris
means that she visited Paris at some
point in the past and now she's back in
the UK careful with pronunciation when
we say bean in a sentence it becomes bin
she's been to Paris but she's gone to
Paris means she traveled to Paris and
she's still in Paris now she's on
holiday she's gone to Paris easy right
bean you go somewhere and return gone
you go somewhere but don't return
